text: T_B: Does a Gaussian density packet on Mcs (N(x,0)=2.0*exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), phi_x=0.3, u=N*phi_x) emit >=2 bright solitons by T=6 under focusing B-NLS? Need final N with >=2 peaks of amplitude >=1.0, mass drift <5%.

method: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step on Psi=sqrt(N+eps_mad)*exp(i*phi_tilde) with phi=0.3*x+phi_tilde split, kinetic exp(-i k^2 dt/2), nonlinear exp(-i kappa |Psi|^2 dt). Sign convention: standard NLS (-1/2)Psi_xx adopted (see kb-nls-sign-convention caveat). Nx=256, dt=1e-3. 2/3 dealiasing on |Psi|^2 and on linear step. Save 25 snapshots.
rejects_bank: NLS:kb-nls-antiperiodic-basis-dark-soliton (IC is genuinely periodic, no phase singularity)
NLS:kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton (no shock in u; u=0.3*N is smooth)
BKdV:kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass (KdV-specific dispersion, doesn't apply to NLS kinetic)
BKdV:kb-gardner-* (no cubic 3/2 v^2 v_x; B-NLS cubic is different)
BKdV:kb-burgers-* (no Burgers shock in u initial condition)
bank_use_rationale: kb-nls-recommended-default-bnls explicitly recommends Strang split-step on Psi for smooth focusing-NLS Gaussian IC. Direct (N,phi) integration is structurally unstable (kb-nls-direct-n-phi-structural-failure: 5 stress tests confirm family-level failure), so 'simplest meaningful' baseline is Madelung-Psi. kb-nls-split-linear-phase requires splitting phi=0.3*x+phi_tilde because phi_0 is non-periodic. BKdV bank entries are mostly irrelevant: no Burgers shock here, no KdV dispersion, no Gardner cubic. Only BKdV dealiasing wisdom transfers and is corroborated by NLS bank's kb-nls-23-dealiasing-cubic.

interpretation: Run completed T=6 with no NaN. Mass conserved to 1e-5 (well within <5% target). Two well-separated peaks of amplitude ~2.16 at the final snapshot — phenomenon target met. Trajectory shows breather-like behaviour: initial Gaussian focuses to N_max=13 around t=1.5, splits into ~5 lobes, refocuses to N_max=13 at t~5, then splits into 2 peaks at T=6. HOWEVER: kb-nls-resolution-soliton-counting warns that Nx=256/dt=1e-3 silently over/under-counts peaks at A>=2; observed spectral tail 1e-1 in upper third during peak focusing far exceeds the 1e-4 threshold flagged as untrustworthy. Therefore peak count = 2 might be a discretization artifact rather than the true converged value.
next_action: E2: increase Nx to 512 (single component upgrade per progressive-complexity discipline). dt remains 1e-3 to satisfy linear-step phase budget; re-check convergence of peak count.

rationale: E1 satisfied the phenomenon target operationally but the spectral-tail diagnostic is a kb-nls-mass-conservation-not-sufficient red flag. Per progressive-complexity rules, upgrade single component (Nx 256 -> 512). dt stays at 1e-3 (well within CFL: phase budget 0.36 at 256, will become 1.43 at 512 — borderline, so reduce dt to 5e-4 per kb-nls-strang-splitstep-bright-soliton recommended_action for A>=2 Gaussian). This is two simultaneous changes only if dt change were independent; treating Nx-and-its-CFL-tied-dt as a single grid-refinement component (the bank tells us they are tied via CFL).

method: Same Madelung-Psi Strang split-step, but Nx=512 and dt=5e-4 (single grid-refinement upgrade, CFL-tied). 2/3 dealiasing kept. After integration, downsample (every 2nd point) to (n_snap, 3, 256) for prediction output to match spec.
rejects_bank: BKdV bank entries: not consulted for this refinement (purely an NLS resolution issue, BKdV has no analog NLS-MI-focusing diagnostic chain)
bank_use_rationale: kb-nls-resolution-soliton-counting recommends Nx>=1024,dt<=2.5e-4 on L=60 for converged amplitude sweep, but T_B's domain is L=30 (half), so the effective dx at Nx=512 here (dx=0.0586) matches their dx=0.0586 on L=60 with Nx=1024. So Nx=512 here is the equivalent of their Nx=1024 standard. kb-nls-cfl-split-step phase budget pi^2*Nx^2*dt/(2L^2) at Nx=512,dt=5e-4 = 0.72 <=1 (just within CFL).

diagnostics.agreement_with_E1: peak positions identical to 3 decimal places; amplitudes match to 4 decimal places; max_N within 0.03 across run

interpretation: Refining to Nx=512/dt=5e-4 reproduces E1 to machine precision in the peak diagnostics. Mass drift improved from -6.4e-6 to -1.4e-11. Spectral tail (90-95%) dropped from 1.2e-3 to 6.5e-7, well below the 1e-4 untrustworthy threshold. Therefore the E1 result of 2 peaks at the final time IS converged. The dynamics are a focusing-NLS Gaussian breather: initial Gaussian (A=2) compresses to a single peak of N=13 at t~1.5 (modulational-instability focusing), then splits/disperses into 2-3 lobes, refocuses at t~5, and at T=6 lands in a state with two well-separated peaks of amplitude ~2.16 — meeting the phenomenon target. The Burgers boost (phi_x=0.3) translates the packet but does not alter the focusing threshold; this matches kb-nls-karpman-maslov-upper-bound's hint that at A=2, T=6 yields 1-2 lobes operationally.
next_action: STOP. Phenomenon target met, result converged.

rationale: E2 confirms convergence: peak structure and amplitudes agree with E1 to <0.01% even though E1's spectral tail flagged under-resolution. The phenomenon target (>=2 peaks with N>=1, mass drift <5%) is achieved with mass drift at machine precision. Spending the third iteration on yet another refinement (Nx=1024) is not justified — both E1 and E2 agree on the answer, so the answer is robust. Final solver = E2 (Madelung-Psi Strang at Nx=512/dt=5e-4 with 2/3 dealias, downsampled to 256 for output).
